name: Vegan Thai Green Curry
intro: A vegetarian curry that will satisfy even the most die-hard meat eaters. Rich in umami and vegetables, which are chosen for its chewy, satisfying texture. Not forgetting the secret ingredient that adds firmness and bright color to store-bought curry. For a gluten-free curry, add gluten-free soy sauce.
method: To make the curry paste, place the leafy greens, curry paste, miso, and mushroom into a blender and well.
Heat ¾ cup of coconut milk over medium heat in a heavy-bottomed pot for about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium and add curry paste (Step 1). Stir for 3-5 minutes.
Add remaining coconut milk and water and let it boil.
Add fried tofu, bamboo shoots, winter melon, palm sugar and soy sauce.
Shred the makrut lime leaves, then add them into the pot. Simmer the curry for about 9 minutes
Add the red peppers and let it boil for 1 more minute. When done, turn off the stove, stir in the fresh basil and let it wilt in the curry.
Serve with jasmine rice immediately, or later (like most Thai curries, it tastes even better the next day!)
